sentences.korean: 안녕하세요 여러분. 오늘도 모두 좋은 하루 보내셨나요?
sentences.english: hello everyone. Did everyone have a good day today?
I talked to a Korean friend today.
It's been a while since I talked to that friend.
We went to the same university in Korea.
We talked a lot about old times.
I attended university in Korea for two years.
After that, I attended university in the United States for four years.
Time passed very quickly.
From now on, I will tell you what college life in Korea was like.
First of all, undergraduate school was very important in Korea.
I was an English undergraduate, English major.

sentences.korean: 영어 학부 안에서 다양한 동아리와 행사가 있었어요.
sentences.english: There were various clubs and events within the English department.

sentences.korean: 댄스, 영화, 사진 동아리 같은 동아리들이요.
sentences.english: Clubs like dance, film, and photography clubs.

sentences.korean: 그래서 학부 안에서 친구들을 많이 만들었어요.
sentences.english: So I made a lot of friends within the department.
If we were in the same department, we knew each other.
And there were undergraduate clothes.

sentences.korean: 야구, 재킷인데 다 같이 그 재킷을 입었어요.
sentences.english: It was a baseball jacket, and we all wore those jackets together.

sentences.korean: 그래서 재킷을 보고 어느 학부인지 알 수 있었어요.
sentences.english: So I was able to tell which department it was by looking at the jacket.

sentences.korean: 하지만 다른 학부의 사람들은 거의 못 만났어요. 별로 친해질 수 없었어요.
sentences.english: But I barely met people from other faculties. We couldn't become very close.
And in Korea, school year was also important.
This is my 4th year at university, with grades ranging from 1st to 4th years.
It was very important who were seniors and juniors.

sentences.korean: 제가 후배일 땐 선배를 존중해야 했어요.
sentences.english: When I was a junior, I had to respect my seniors.

sentences.korean: 제가 선배를 땐 후배를 잘 챙겨줘야 했어요.
sentences.english: When I was a senior, I had to take good care of my juniors.
I had to remember people well.

sentences.korean: 다음으로 한국에서는 운전하는 친구가 거의 없었어요.
sentences.english: Next, I had almost no friends who drove in Korea.
At first, I lived in the school dormitory.

sentences.korean: 그래서 쉽게 걸어다닐 수 있었어요. 그러다 마지막에는 학교 밖에서 살았어요.
sentences.english: So I was able to walk around easily. Then, in the end, I lived outside of school.
Although the school changed, it was close to the school.
A university bus came to our neighborhood.
I often came at the same time every day.

sentences.korean: 아침에 버스를 타러 일찍 나가야 했어요.
sentences.english: I had to leave early to catch the bus in the morning.
Many people waited for the bus.
The waiting line was very long.
So I had to get ready quickly and go out.

sentences.korean: 학교에 운전해서 오는 선배가 한명 밖에 없었어요.
sentences.english: There was only one senior who drove to school.

sentences.korean: 모두 버스나 지하철을 타거나 걸어서 학교에 왔어요. 집에 가거나 놀러 갈 때도 버스와 지하철을 탔고요.
sentences.english: Everyone came to school by bus, subway, or walking. When I went home or went to play, I took the bus and subway.

sentences.korean: 학교에 주차장에는 선생님들의 차 밖에 없었어요.
sentences.english: There were only teachers' cars in the school parking lot.

sentences.korean: 또 저는 대학교에서 아주 많은 시간을 보냈어요.
sentences.english: Also, I spent a lot of time at university.

sentences.korean: 한 학기에 보통 두 번 시험을 봤어요. 시험 기간이 다가오면 학교가 바빠져요.
sentences.english: I usually took the exam twice a semester. School gets busy as exam period approaches.
The school library is open until midnight.
Many people studied in the school library until 12 o'clock.
I also often went to the library with my friends.
We studied together until late at night.

sentences.korean: 학교에서 친구들과 밤을 샌 적도 있어요.
sentences.english: There was a time when I stayed up all night with my friends from school.

sentences.korean: 같이 공부하고 저녁과 간식을 사 먹고 또 공부했어요.
sentences.english: We studied together, bought dinner and snacks, and studied again.

sentences.korean: 그리고 다음 날 아침에 집에 가서 샤워하고 다시 학교에 왔어요.
sentences.english: And the next morning, I went home, took a shower, and came back to school.

sentences.korean: 밤에 먹는 맥도날드 햄버거는 아주 맛있었어요.
sentences.english: The McDonald's hamburger I ate at night was very delicious.
Of course, studying all night was difficult.
I don't want to do it again.
